name: Niklaas
classification_notes: Niklaas is a COLR/CPAL and SVG color font with multiple chromatic styles, placing it firmly in the display category as a decorative, technically specialised typeface designed for visual impact rather than extended text setting.
summary: A COLR/CPAL and SVG color font from Typearture, offered in three chromatic styles with a bonus Ruby variant in the complete package.
description: Niklaas is a color font using both COLR/CPAL and SVG technologies, requiring compatibility checks before use. Three named styles, White, Milk and Pure, are sold individually or as a complete bundle that adds a bonus Ruby style. Coverage spans Basic Latin, Western European, Central European and South European character sets across 361 glyphs.
foundry_blurb: Typearture describe Niklaas as a COLR/CPAL and SVG color font available in White, Milk and Pure styles, with the Complete package adding a bonus Ruby style. The family covers 361 characters across Basic Latin and several European language sets, and Typearture advise buyers to check software and system support before purchasing.

name: Schijn
classification_notes: Schijn is a display typeface with heavily stylised letterforms modelled on faceted gemstones, making it an ornamental decorative display font with no conventional body text application.
summary: A gemstone-themed display typeface in two styles, Cut and Rough, with colour font and variable font versions tracing the full journey of a faceted stone from mine to market.
description: Schijn divides into two distinct styles: Cut, with 16 styles across Shallow, Middle and Deep cuts, and Rough, with 9 styles evoking unpolished stone. The family includes dingbats, small capitals, old style numerals and stylistic alternates across 580 characters, with European Latin language support throughout. The Complete package adds colour font versions of both Cut and Rough styles, plus a variable font version of Cut.
foundry_blurb: Typearture describe Schijn as a gemstone font unlike others, one that encompasses the entire process of a faceted gemstone from mining to cutting to sale. The letterforms and dingbats are designed to reveal the gap between exertion and extravagance, adding a layer of storytelling that makes Schijn more than a decorative novelty.

name: Fabel
classification_notes: Fabel is a display typeface in which each letterform is constructed from an animal illustration, making it a pictorial or novelty face rather than a conventional text or titling type.
summary: An animal alphabet typeface in which each letterform is built around an actual animal, covering a full Latin character set across three styles: Line, Inner and Filled.
description: Rather than retrofitting tails and whiskers onto an existing typeface, Fabel constructs every letter around its corresponding animal, letting the creature's natural form dictate the shape. The family spans 330 characters from the A of Anteater to the Z of Zander, including upper and lower case, punctuation, old-style and tabular numerals, and a broad range of mathematical symbols. Three styles, Line, Inner and Filled, give designers control over visual weight and detail level.
foundry_blurb: Typearture describe Fabel as an animal alphabet that lets the animal dictate the letter shape rather than adapting an existing font. Each creature is shown at its best, wings spread, feathers ruffled and claws sharp, covering Earth's wildlife diversity across a complete Latin character set with three styles, punctuation, numerals and mathematical symbols.

name: Disclosure Dingbats
classification_notes: A dingbat typeface containing pictorial symbols and illustrations rather than letterforms, offered in illustration and icon styles optimised for display and body text use respectively.
summary: A dingbat typeface with 666 characters spanning pointing hands, clocks, vehicles and famous buildings, inspired by Wingdings conspiracy lore.
description: Two styles, Illustration and Icon, are optimised as companions for display and body text respectively, each drawing from the same set of 666 characters. OpenType ligatures (129 in total) and 3,500 keywords drive the behaviour, causing the symbols to shift as you type and reveal conspiratorial sequences. The concept is rooted in the infamous Wingdings prophecies, reframing that tradition as a playful but carefully constructed system.
foundry_blurb: Typearture describe Disclosure Dingbats as a dingbat typeface filled with a diverse range of subjects, from pointing hands to clocks and vehicles to famous buildings. Inspired by the infamous Wingdings prophecies, the typeface changes as you type, revealing conspiracy secrets that may even tell things to come. Each dingbat comes in two styles, Illustration and Icon, suited to display or body text, with 666 characters and 129 ligatures across 3,500 keywords.

name: Pegacorn Initials
classification_notes: Ornate hand-drawn initials depicting unicorns and Pegasus, designed exclusively for large display sizes; the illustrative, figurative construction places it firmly in decorative display territory.
summary: A set of hand-drawn decorative initials depicting unicorns and Pegasus, offered in line and filled styles for large-scale display use.
description: Each glyph in the full Latin alphabet is individually drawn with illustrative detail: flowing manes, feathered wings and mythological references drawn from popular culture and legend. The family ships with 357 characters including basic diacritics, a punctuation and symbol set, tabular figures and 71 custom illustrations. Intended for use at 48 points or above, where the fine linework and figurative detail can be properly read.
foundry_blurb: Typearture describe Pegacorn Initials as a highly detailed set of initials based on unicorns and Pegasus, with each character drawn by hand with careful attention to lush manes and feathered wings. Available in Line and Filled styles, the set covers a full Latin alphabet with basic diacritics, punctuation, tabular figures and 71 custom illustrations, and is designed for display use at 48 points or above.

name: Magical Unicorn Neue Pro
classification_notes: A novelty decorative typeface built around a unicorn motif, featuring chromatic colour fonts and variable experimental versions; firmly in the display/decorative category with no conventional text use intended.
summary: A free novelty display family with colour font and variable capabilities, built around a unicorn theme across eight distinct styles.
description: The family spans eight styles: line variants in Horn and Sans cuts, colour palette versions in Color and Noire, and two experimental variable styles including a variable colour version. Colour font support and variability make it technically more ambitious than most novelty typefaces of its kind. Distributed free for all use including commercial, covering Basic Latin and Central and Western European character sets.
foundry_blurb: Typearture describe Magical Unicorn Neue Pro as a new standard of unicorn typefaces: all-usable, all-encompassing, colourful and variable when it matters most. The eight styles include two colour palettes with two variants each, two line variants, and two variable experimental versions, with a 279-character set and a free commercial licence.
